perturbation.original_text: (c) During the Term, without the Company's written consent, Principal shall not, either in his individual capacity or as an agent for another: (i) hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents; (ii) entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; (iii) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; or (iv) contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) During the Term, Principal shall have the right to hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents; and entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; and contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; and contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the restriction requiring 'Company's written consent' for Principal to solicit employees/customers. This directly contradicts the original clause in section 2(c) and undermining the protections intended for Company and PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) During the Term, Principal shall have the right to hire or offer to hire any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents; and entice away or in any other manner persuade or attempt to persuade any of Company's, Phoenix Footwear's or PFG Acquisition's officers, employees, or agents to discontinue their relationship with Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition; and contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any business whatsoever by influencing or attempting to influence any customer of Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition with whom Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition has engaged in sales discussions prior to the termination of this Agreement; and contract, solicit, divert, or attempt to divert from Company, Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition any supplier or vendor.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts Principal from hiring or soliciting employees/customers without the Company's written consent. The changed text removes this restriction, explicitly granting Principal the right to hire and solicit without consent. This creates a direct conflict with the original intent and meaning of the agreement, thus contradicting the original clause. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text which means that in one place in the contract it states the principal shall NOT hire/solicit and in another place (the Changed Text) it states that the principal SHALL hire/solicit. This is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Principal covenants and agrees that he shall not, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, without the prior written consent of Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Principal covenants and agrees that he shall, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the restriction requiring the 'prior written consent of Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition' for Principal to use or disclose Confidential Information. This contradicts the original clause, potentially undermining the confidentiality protections intended for Phoenix Footwear and PFG Acquisition. There is ambiguity about Principal's obligation regarding Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Principal covenants and agrees that he shall, at any time, during the Restrictions Period, directly or indirectly, use, exploit, or disclose to any person or entity, any Confidential Information, except as expressly authorized by Phoenix Footwear or PFG Acquisition during the performance of Principal's duties for and with PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'prior written consent' for using or disclosing Confidential Information, while the changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This removal directly contradicts the original intent of protecting Confidential Information, as the Principal no longer needs permission to use it during the Restrictions Period, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: PRINCIPAL HAS CAREFULLY READ AND CONSIDERED THE PROVISIONS OF SECTIONS 2 AND 3 HEREOF AND, HAVING DONE SO, HEREBY AGREES THAT THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH IN SUCH SECTIONS ARE FAIR AND REASONABLE AND ARE REASONABLY REQUIRED FOR THE PROTECTION OF THE INTERESTS OF PHOENIX FOOTWEAR AND PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: PRINCIPAL HAS CAREFULLY READ AND CONSIDERED THE PROVISIONS OF SECTIONS 2 AND 3 HEREOF AND, HAVING DONE SO, HEREBY DISAGREES THAT THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH IN SUCH SECTIONS ARE FAIR AND REASONABLE AND ARE REASONABLY REQUIRED FOR THE PROTECTION OF THE INTERESTS OF PHOENIX FOOTWEAR AND PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating Principal disagrees with the fairness and reasonableness of the restrictions. This contradicts the initial statement in section 4 of the agreement. It creates uncertainty about Principal's actual agreement to the restrictions and undermines the enforceability of those restrictions. It contradicts the binding nature of the non-compete.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PRINCIPAL HAS CAREFULLY READ AND CONSIDERED THE PROVISIONS OF SECTIONS 2 AND 3 HEREOF AND, HAVING DONE SO, HEREBY DISAGREES THAT THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH IN SUCH SECTIONS ARE FAIR AND REASONABLE AND ARE REASONABLY REQUIRED FOR THE PROTECTION OF THE INTERESTS OF PHOENIX FOOTWEAR AND PFG Acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Principal *disagrees* with the fairness and reasonableness of the restrictions in sections 2 and 3. This directly contradicts the purpose of the original clause, which was to affirm the Principal's agreement with those restrictions. The Contradicted Text restates the Changed Text, thus it is in direct opposition of the original, which would assume the Principal does agree with the restrictions.
